title: rosa coriifolia cinerea
intro: Rosa coriifolia cinerea, also known as the Ashy Hawthorn, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its grayish leaves and small, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. Rosa coriifolia cinerea has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of fever and infection.
